The GH Cup

Our flagship annual moot court competition.

The GH Cup 2026

The Guelph-Humber Pre-Law Society proudly hosts The GH Cup, an annual moot court competition that challenges participants to develop and present compelling legal arguments before a panel of judges. Open to undergraduate students across Ontario.

Stay Updated

Compete

Test your advocacy skills against teams from universities across the province in a structured moot format.

Judge

Law students, lawyers, and legal professionals are invited to volunteer as judges and provide feedback.

Network

Connect with peers, mentors, and legal professionals in a competitive yet collaborative environment.

What is The GH Cup?

The GH Cup is an annual inter-university moot court competition organized by the Guelph-Humber Pre-Law Society. Competitors present both written submissions (skeleton arguments) and oral advocacy before a panel of judges drawn from the legal profession.

The competition provides a valuable opportunity for undergraduate students to develop the skills they'll need in law school and beyond, while networking with peers and legal professionals from across the province.

How It Works

  • Teams of two compete on behalf of either the appellant or respondent
  • A case problem is released in advance for preparation
  • Competitors submit written skeleton arguments before the oral rounds
  • Oral rounds are judged by volunteer lawyers, law students, and professors
  • Awards are given for best oral advocates and best written submissions
